title: Cloud Run Revisions, Validation, Traffic, Rollback, and Recovery · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for cloud run revisions, validation, traffic, rollback, and recovery and requires the learner to defend the smallest safe progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Deploy one verified image digest with explicit service, region, configuration, runtime identity, revision suffix, labels, and no-traffic option.
Validate revision readiness, configuration, identity, logs, authenticated smoke behavior, changed inputs, and dependency failure before traffic.
Move bounded traffic by revision or tag, define observation window, compare user and system signals, and stop on explicit abort conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Ci Deployment Iam Separation
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Cloud Run release rebuilds after approval, sends all traffic before readiness, changes IAM, and cannot restore the prior digest. Deploy no-traffic, verify revision, canary signals, abort, rollback, and identity separation. Competency probe: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Ci Deployment Iam Separation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for keep application deployment separate from public access and iam mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Ci Deployment Iam Separation, keep Ci Deployment Iam Separation connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A quiz Cloud Run release rebuilds after approval, sends all traffic before readiness, changes IAM, and cannot restore the prior digest. Deploy no-traffic, verify revision, canary signals, abort, rollback, and identity separation. Competency probe: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Ci Deployment Iam Separation symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Google Cloud Run Deployment, Revisions, Traffic, and Rollback — Controls revision identity, no-traffic deployment, readiness, tags, gradual traffic, rollback, IAM, service identity, and recovery.

At Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Ci Deployment Iam Separation, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Cloud Run release rebuilds after approval, sends all traffic before readiness, changes IAM, and cannot restore the prior digest. Deploy no-traffic, verify revision, canary signals, abort, rollback, and identity separation. Competency probe: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.

For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Ci Deployment Iam Separation, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: The deployment workflow should automatically make a service public for convenience.

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.
